author: Sadok Merkikou
msg:
Good morning Unity family.

I had an idea, and perhaps you could share it with the team.
You know, like me, that people love the word "free."
But if I had to tell each of my ULOs that they have to buy credits for their lease at a later date, they'd start complaining again.
Isn't it a good idea to let people earn money and then have them pay for the credits based on what they've earned?
People see that as free money, and yet they'll pay for it without a problem.
Perhaps it's also an idea to change something in the application.
A pop-up for the UNO before issuing a license.
A pop-up asking whether they or the ULO will pay for the credits.
If these are transferred to the ULO, a question will pop up asking, "Pay for your credits first to use your license." Of course, this is all for a later date, when people have to buy the credits to activate their licenses, but it's better to think about it now than later.

I'll hear from people if they think it's a good idea or not.

Thanks for your feedback.

author: A
msg:
Its pretty simple in my view why it wont happen until after round 2 is almost over:

- They changed terms and conditions in the beginning so nodes cant be sold until all nodes are sold or 6 months after sale. 

- The community made them delete that. 

- But they clearly dont want anyone to sell their nodes for 9K when round 2 starts at 10K. Thats why they added the terms in the first place.

- So now that the community made them delete that term, the only option to prevent cheaper sales in round 2 is to delay eth mint until after round 2 or 6 months after the sale like the terms were changed. 

In short they be like: Ok we change the terms back so you can sell your node right away, but we will delay eth mint so you can only sell on WMC were there will be almost no buyers. 

Im not speaking for or against it. For me personally I dont see a reason to sell my node before round 2 is over as the value will double from 5 to 10K. But thats the only logic i can see for eth minting to be delayed and ignored.

That’s only partly true. The Terms were not „deleted“. They have been lifted for buys prior to the change. Terms still apply to buyers after the term was introduced as far as I know.

So if you bought before a certain date, you could sell/transfer your NFT, after that date, you can’t.

I might be wrong, but this is my knowledge.

author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: I've tested both Secure Folder on Samsung and Private Space on Google. Simple to set up, reliable in use. They let you keep a UNO login version of the app inside the folder—totally separate—and still run your licensed node-operator version outside, at the same time. Handy because it means no downtime; if you do this on multiple phones it makes for a great backup if the main phone dies or you don't want to travel with your hardware wallet to log into your UNO version. This way, every single phone can earn its keep. Recommended.

author: MSA | Valhalla.Node
msg:
No, Unity can not help keeping X online. At least not in the way it's meant to work currently, and if it ever can, it'll be in a very indirect way. It's testing telecoms routes, it's not changing anything. So best case scenario, it can help detect issues, but the client would still need to fix those issues themselves.

afaik...

author: Nico | NodeX
msg:
List of summary tasks I got from the AMAs...

🔍 Commercial Tasks Prepared
These include advanced telecom verification and quality checks such as:
Call route integrity checks
SMS delivery verification
Caller ID & Sender ID consistency
DLR (delivery receipt) reports
Telecom latency verification
Audio quality scoring (MOS-like testing)

🚀 Next Deployment Phase   (Scout & Runner?)

Telecom environment checks  
Multi-route comparisons across carriers
Increased utilization of SIM + Wi-Fi networks

🧠 New Technology & Task Families

Unity is also developing Wi-Fi network testing technology and has already prepared 10–15 new task families, including:
Multi-hop path analysis
Dual-device synchronized testing
Environment-based verification
Anomaly triangulation
Fraud pattern detection
ISP-level routing behavior analysis
Hybrid Wi-Fi & cellular tasks
Timing & sequence-based testing
Regional pattern verification
Deeper connectivity checks

author: BeamNode
msg:

author: Elitemas77
msg: Check out “tangem pay”. In a tangem wallet you can get a visa card and pay for anything, any place that accepts visa. I think you can even withdraw from an atm. It uses only USDC on the polygon network to pay, so you will have to move your rewards to that.
